title: AI-Powered Event Discovery for Buenos Aires
description: Turned fragmented Buenos Aires event data into a live discovery product that helps people find plans through grounded AI search, not another dead-end chat demo.
bestFit: Teams exploring AI assistants, RAG search, or data-heavy discovery products that need grounded answers.
constraints: Event data was fragmented across multiple sources with inconsistent formats.
The assistant needed to answer vibe-based queries without drifting away from real event data.
problem: Buenos Aires has a thriving nightlife and cultural scene, but event information is scattered across incompatible platforms: Instagram stories, Facebook events, Eventbrite, Passline, venue websites, and WhatsApp groups. There's no single source of truth, making it nearly impossible to discover what's happening tonight.

Traditional search doesn't work for events. Users want to ask questions like 'romantic jazz spots in Palermo' or 'underground techno this weekend' - queries that require understanding intent, vibes, and context rather than just keyword matching.
solution: Created a conversational discovery experience so users could ask for events in plain language instead of hunting across Instagram, ticketing sites, and venue feeds.
Turned scattered event sources into a cleaner operating dataset by ingesting events, matching venues, enriching descriptions, and keeping the catalog usable.
Combined semantic search with structured filters so the product could handle intent-heavy queries like mood, neighborhood, or time window instead of just keywords.
Added the admin and data-quality tooling needed to keep answers grounded in real event data rather than turning into an unreliable AI demo.
result: Launched a working event discovery product with 800+ events and 190+ venues pulled into one searchable experience instead of forcing users to jump between sources.
Proved a repeatable pattern for AI-assisted discovery over messy local data: ingest it, clean it, enrich it, embed it, and answer with grounded results.
The product is now a live example of how I build AI systems that need trustworthy retrieval, admin tooling, and a usable product around the model, not just a chat box.
